title: fix(release): only tag the highest semver release as `latest`
body:
Motivation:
The `latest` and `latest-mariner` Docker tags (on Docker Hub and GitHub
Packages) are meant to point to the newest release by semantic version, but
they were being applied to whichever release was published most recently in
chronological order. `.github/scripts/get_release_version.py` set
`LATEST_RELEASE=true` whenever a release-notes file existed for the tag being
built, with no version comparison. As a result, a hotfix to an older release
branch (for example publishing 1.9.6 after 1.10.0) would overwrite the
`latest` tag and point users at an older release.

Approach:
Before writing `LATEST_RELEASE=true`, verify the version being built is the
highest one across all `docs/release_notes/v*.md` files. Two small stdlib-only
helpers are added: `parseVersion` (parses a final `major.minor.patch` version
to an int tuple, returning None for pre-releases so rc/alpha notes are
ignored) and `isLatestRelease` (returns true only when no existing release
note has a strictly greater version). Integer-tuple comparison also fixes the
related `1.10 < 1.9` string-ordering trap. No new dependencies are introduced
(the `packaging` library is intentionally avoided as it is not installed
before this step runs), keeping the change fork-safe.

Validation:
Ran the script from the repo root across scenarios:
  GITHUB_REF=refs/tags/v1.18.2  -> LATEST_RELEASE=true  (highest release)
  GITHUB_REF=refs/tags/v1.9.5   -> LATEST_RELEASE unset  (old-branch hotfix)
  GITHUB_REF=refs/tags/v1.19.0-rc.1 -> rc path unchanged, no LATEST_RELEASE
  missing notes file            -> exits 1 (behavior unchanged)
Also exercised the helpers directly (parseVersion/isLatestRelease) against the
real `docs/release_notes/` directory and a simulated "1.9.6 after 1.10.0"
directory; all assertions passed and `python3 -m py_compile` succeeds.

RELEASE NOTE: **FIX** `latest` Docker tag no longer regresses to an older release when a hotfix is published on an older branch.

Fixes #5625

Signed-off-by: Pujitha Paladugu <10557236+pujitha24@users.noreply.github.com>

comments.body:
`LATEST_RELEASE` is used by the release workflow for more than just the Docker `latest` tag (e.g., selecting the GitHub release body in `.github/workflows/dapr.yml:538-542` and gating Helm chart packaging in `.github/workflows/dapr.yml:715-723`). With the new logic, a final but non-highest hotfix release (e.g., v1.9.6 after v1.10.0) will no longer set `LATEST_RELEASE=true`, which can cause those steps to treat the release like an RC and/or skip publishing artifacts.

Consider keeping `LATEST_RELEASE=true` to mean “final (non-rc/alpha) release”, and introducing a separate env var (e.g., `HIGHEST_SEMVER_RELEASE`/`TAG_LATEST`) that is set only when the version is the highest semver; then update the Docker tagging logic to use the new var.

files.patch:
@@ -16,12 +16,41 @@
 # LATEST_RELEASE to true to add 'latest' tag to docker image.
 
 import os
+import re
 import sys
+import glob
 import datetime
 
 gitRef = os.getenv("GITHUB_REF")
 tagRefPrefix = "refs/tags/v"
 
+
+# parseVersion returns a (major, minor, patch) tuple of ints for a final
+# release version such as "1.9.5". It returns None for pre-release versions
+# (e.g. "1.0.0-rc.1") or anything it cannot parse, so that only final releases
+# are considered when determining the 'latest' tag.
+def parseVersion(version):
+    match = re.fullmatch(r"(\d+)\.(\d+)\.(\d+)", version)
+    if match is None:
+        return None
+    return tuple(int(part) for part in match.groups())
+
+
+# isLatestRelease returns True only if the given final release version is the
+# highest one across all release notes in releaseNotesDir. This prevents a
+# hotfix to an older release branch (e.g. 1.9.6 published after 1.10.0) from
+# being tagged as 'latest' just because it is the most recent chronologically.
+def isLatestRelease(version, releaseNotesDir):
+    current = parseVersion(version)
+    if current is None:
+        return False
+    for notePath in glob.glob(os.path.join(releaseNotesDir, "v*.md")):
+        # Strip the leading 'v' and trailing '.md' from the file name.
+        other = parseVersion(os.path.basename(notePath)[1:-3])
+        if other is not None and other > current:
+            return False
+    return True
+
 with open(os.getenv("GITHUB_ENV"), "a") as githubEnv:
 
     if "schedule" in sys.argv:
@@ -47,8 +76,13 @@
         print ("Checking if {} exists".format(releaseNotePath))
         if os.path.exists(releaseNotePath):
             print ("Found {}".format(releaseNotePath))
-            # Set LATEST_RELEASE to true
-            githubEnv.write("LATEST_RELEASE=true\n")
+            # Only tag as 'latest' if this is the highest release version, so
+            # that hotfixes to older branches do not overwrite the 'latest' tag.
+            if isLatestRelease(releaseVersion, os.path.dirname(releaseNotePath)):
+                print ("{} is the latest release; setting LATEST_RELEASE=true".format(releaseVersion))
+                githubEnv.write("LATEST_RELEASE=true\n")
+            else:
+                print ("{} is not the latest release; skipping 'latest' tag".format(releaseVersion))
         else:
             print ("{} is not found".format(releaseNotePath))
             sys.exit(1)
